Implemented + validated (typecheck 0 errors, oxlint clean). Committing.

Added collision-resolution pass to incrementalLayout: new nodes are nudged clear of pinned nodes (least-penetration phase 1 + drop-below phase 2 guarantee), pinned nodes never move. Unit-tested (11 pass), lint/type clean, adversarially reviewed; consumer path after saved graphLayout confirmed reached.

Memoized useGraphModel (graph nodes/edges/traffic derived per-render, rebuilt 60fps during drag) so liveNodes/liveEdges keep stable refs when data is unchanged; and fixed the mid-drag branch in graph/layout.tsx to only mint a new node object for the node whose position actually changed. Result: dragging re-renders only the dragged card, not the whole graph. Tests 16/16, tsc clean, no new lint errors (pre-existing max-lines/complexity untouched).

Wrapped ResourceNode in memo with a comparator over the only props the renderers read (id, data, selected, dragging). React Flow re-renders the dragged node's wrapper each frame to apply its transform; without memo that re-ran each card's useMutation/tooltip hooks 60fps. Now the dragged card moves via the wrapper transform without re-rendering its contents. tsc 0, lint 0, graph tests 16/16.

Drag no longer routes per-frame through React state. onNodesChange stopped calling setDragged each frame; mid-drag laidOutNodes returns the exact same frozen nodes array reference so React Flow's own store owns the motion and no reconcile/re-render fires per frame. Resting position(s) committed to dragged+layoutCache and persisted once in onNodeDragStop (multi-select aware via the nodes arg), batched with setDragging(false) so the canvas re-renders exactly once at rest. tsc 0, graph tests 16/16, no new lint. Needs an in-browser drag smoke-test (cursor tracking).

Reworked: the merged-position separation now treats operator-dragged nodes as FIXED (isMovable = !dragged.has(id)). A card you drag stays exactly where you drop it (fixes the 'resolves back' spring); only auto-placed nodes (never manually positioned) are nudged clear of overlaps. Two hand-placed cards may overlap if dropped that way — operator's choice. Removed the aggressive resolveOverlaps (moved everyone). layout-graph tests 13/13, my files type-clean, lint only pre-existing max-lines.
